⏺ Two issues were causing the imports to fail silently:

  1. Missing description column in the media_files table — the table was created
   before we added AI descriptions, and CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS doesn't
  update existing tables. Fixed with the migration.
  2. Blob "already exists" error — partial imports uploaded images to Blob but
  failed at the DB insert, then retries couldn't overwrite. Fixed with
  addRandomSuffix: false.

  Both are fixed now. Once deployed (~1 min), go back to the Asset Manager,
  switch to the Media Pending tab, and try the batch import again. The 52 deals
  should process correctly this time.

  Also: you still need to enable the Google Drive API in your Google Cloud
  project for the ~10 deals that use Google Drive links. The 80+ WorkDrive deals
   will work now.

  specific URL pattern. For the rest, it falls back to client_assets. But the
  detectSource function might misidentify the source. Let me simplify: always
  prefer whichever URL contains a recognizable source.

  (workdrive.zohoexternal.com/external/HASH) can't be resolved to a folder ID
  via the API. The system falls back to searching by name, but "The Trails
  Matterport Scanning" won't match a folder called "The Trails" in WorkDrive.

  Two options to fix the WD-hash deals:

  1. You manually approve the correct WorkDrive folder link — In the Asset
  Manager, expand the deal, click "Internal Assets" to open the WorkDrive folder
   in your browser, copy the URL from the address bar (it'll be
  workdrive.zoho.com/folder/XXXXX), and I can add a field to paste that in.
  2. I make the name search fuzzier — match partial names, strip words like
  "Photography", "Matterport Scanning", "Inventory", etc.

  Want me to do option 2 and also add an override URL field for the stubborn
  ones?

⏺ Write(src/lib/workdrive.ts)

       61    );                                                          
       62  }                                                             
       63                                                                
       64 +// Strip common suffixes that appear in deal names but not    
          +folder names                                                  
       65 +function normalizeDealName(name: string): string[] {          
       66 +  const stripped = name                                       
       67 +    .replace(/\b(photography|production|photo|matterport|scan 
          +ning|inventory|exterior|interior|model|models|sales           
          +gallery|amenities|clubhouse|twilight|brand                    
          +update|spec\+?|lifestyle)\b/gi, "")                           
       68 +    .replace(/\s+/g, " ")                                     
       69 +    .trim();                                                  
       70 +                                                              
       71 +  const candidates: string[] = [];                            
       72 +                                                              
       73 +  // Full stripped name                                       
       74 +  if (stripped) candidates.push(stripped.toLowerCase());      
       75 +                                                              
       76 +  // Original name                                            
       77 +  candidates.push(name.toLowerCase());                        
       78 +                                                              
       79 +  // First 2-3 significant words                              
       80 +  const words = stripped.split(" ").filter((w) => w.length >  
          +2);                                                           
       81 +  if (words.length >= 2) candidates.push(words.slice(0,       
          +2).join(" ").toLowerCase());                                  
       82 +  if (words.length >= 3) candidates.push(words.slice(0,       
          +3).join(" ").toLowerCase());                                  
       83 +                                                              
       84 +  // Also try without trailing descriptors separated by " - " 
          + or " – "                                                     
       85 +  const dashSplit = name.split(/\s*[-–]\s*/)[0].trim();       
       86 +  if (dashSplit && dashSplit !== name)                        
          +candidates.push(dashSplit.toLowerCase());                     
       87 +                                                              
       88 +  return [...new Set(candidates)];                            
       89 +}                                                             
       90 +                                                              
       91 +// Fuzzy match: does the folder name contain any of the       
          +search candidates?                                            
       92 +function fuzzyMatch(folderName: string, candidates:           
          +string[]): boolean {                                          
       93 +  const lower = folderName.toLowerCase();                     
       94 +  return candidates.some((c) => lower.includes(c) ||          
          +c.includes(lower));                                           
       95 +}                                                             
       96 +                                                              
       97  // Recursively search for a folder matching the deal name     
       98 -// Structure: Finished Assets > Builder > Year > Region >     
          -Deal                                                          
       98  async function findDealFolder(                                
       99    builderName: string,                                        
      100    dealName: string,                                           
      101    accessToken: string,

⏺ Done. Two improvements:

  Fuzzy search — the name matching now strips common suffixes before searching:
  - "The Trails Matterport Scanning" → searches for "The Trails", "Trails"
  - "Pinnacle at Solaire Inventory" → searches for "Pinnacle at Solaire",
  "Pinnacle Solaire"
  - "Arbor Ranch Bungalows/Cottages Sales Gallery" → searches for "Arbor Ranch"

  Override URL — when import fails:
  1. Error message shows what went wrong
  2. A URL input field appears below
  3. Open the deal's "Internal Assets" or "Client Assets" link in your browser
  4. Navigate to the correct folder in WorkDrive
  5. Copy the URL from your browser bar (it'll be
  workdrive.zoho.com/folder/XXXXX)
  6. Paste it in and click Retry

  Try the batch import again. The fuzzy search should catch most of the
  remaining 14. For any that still fail, you'll see the override field to paste
  the correct URL.

⏺ The internal folder is empty. The client folder has subfolders with images.
  But pickAssetUrl prefers internal Google Drive over client. The fix: when
  internal returns 0 images, fall back to client assets.

  The real issue is the URL picker prioritizes internal_assets for Google Drive,
   but these internal folders are often the production workspace (empty after
  delivery), while client_assets has the finished deliverables.

⏺ Here's what changed:

  The import now tries ALL available URLs instead of picking just one:
  1. WorkDrive direct folder ID (most reliable)
  2. Client WorkDrive link
  3. Client Google Drive link
  4. Internal Google Drive link (tried last — often empty)
  5. Internal WorkDrive hash (fallback)

  If the first URL returns 0 images, it automatically tries the next one. This
  fixes the Wayfare Station issue — the internal Google Drive folder was empty,
  but the client folder has the images.

  Fuzzy name matching strips: Photography, Production, Photo, Matterport,
  Scanning, Inventory, Exterior, Interior, Model, Amenities, Clubhouse,
  Twilight, Brand Update, Spec+, Lifestyle, Sales Gallery.

  Try the batch again. Most of those 14 should go through now.

       97 +// Normalize a builder name for searching: strip periods,

       98 +function normalizeBuilder(name: string): string[] {           
       99 +  const base = name.split(",")[0].split(" -                   
          +")[0].split(":")[0].trim();                                   
      100 +  const noPeriods = base.replace(/\./g, "");                  
      101 +  const withPeriods = base.replace(/([A-Z])([A-Z])/g,         
          +"$1.$2."); // DR → D.R.                                       
      102 +  return [...new Set([base, noPeriods, withPeriods].map(s =>  
          +s.toLowerCase().trim()).filter(Boolean))];                    
      103 +}

❯ lets do the copy URL feature. which URL do you need for this to work?         

⏺ I need a workdrive.zoho.com/folder/XXXXX URL.

  When you're browsing WorkDrive in your browser and you open a folder, the URL
  in the address bar will look like:

  https://workdrive.zoho.com/folder/fvzdf171a3a2748c0418abf47eb3816b4e980

  That folder ID at the end is what I need. Paste the full URL into the override
   field on the deal that's failing, click Retry, and it'll pull the images from
   that exact folder.

  the URL. The override field is already there on every failed import.

⏺ Done. Here's the new gallery curation workflow:

  How to curate:
  1. Go to any gallery page (e.g. /work/model-homes)
  2. Click "Manage Gallery" in the admin toolbar
  3. For each assigned project, click the gear icon (⚙) next to the project
  name
  4. The Gallery Curator opens showing all images for that project

  In the Curator:
  - Star icon (⭐) — click to set as the cover image (this image appears as the
  project tile on the gallery page). Purple ring highlights the current cover.
  Click again to unset.
  - Eye icon (👁) — click to hide an image from the gallery. Hidden images show
  a dark overlay with "HIDDEN" label. They won't appear on the public site but
  stay in the media library.
  - Save Changes — persists your selections

  On the public gallery:
  - Cover image shows as the project's thumbnail tile
  - Hidden images are filtered out from both the tile and the lightbox
  - If no cover is set, the first visible image is used

  Font fixes:
  - Nav CTA buttons: 11px → 12px
  - Stats "By the Numbers" label: 10px → 12px

  design. Want me to add a background image field to it?

  section on the homepage with West/Mountain/Central/East placeholder cards.
  These are hardcoded in a section component, not connected to the admin system.
   They need to either:
  - Become swappable via the image editor (add data-slot + DynamicImage)

  /services/premium

  Which would you prefer? I'd suggest:
  1. Add background image support to the "Built For Builders" dark section

  media library (or remove the section entirely since Premium now has its own
  page)

⏺ Done. The homepage now has significantly more visual real estate:

  Parallax backgrounds (dark sections):
  - Built For Builders — full-screen parallax bg slot (bg-built-for-builders),
  65% dark overlay
  - Try FrameFlow — parallax bg slot (bg-try-frameflow), 60% overlay

  -2)
  - Filmstrip — horizontal scrolling strip of 5 images between VirtualServices
  and BuiltForBuilders (home-filmstrip-0 through -4)
  - Masonry grid — 4 images in asymmetric 2-column layout before PremiumGallery
  (home-showcase-2-0 through -3)

  How to populate:
  1. Log into admin, go to homepage
  2. Click "Edit Images"
  3. Click any parallax background or showcase slot
  4. Pick from your media library or upload new

  All slots start empty (subtle light placeholder). As you add images, the
  homepage transforms from text-heavy to photo-forward.

✻ Cogitated for 5m 53s

❯ analyze what you just did, and tell me why it doesnt work                     

⏺ Fetch(https://daviesimaging.vercel.app)
  ⎿  Received 56KB (200 OK)

⏺ The problem is obvious: I added a bunch of image showcase sections that have
  no images assigned yet. So the homepage now has large empty gray placeholder
  blocks between the content sections. It looks broken, not photo-forward.

  Three issues:

  1. Empty showcases visible — The trio, filmstrip, and masonry sections render
  as blank gray rectangles because no one has assigned images to
  home-showcase-1-0, home-filmstrip-0, etc.
  2. Empty parallax backgrounds — BuiltForBuilders, TryFrameFlow, FinalCta all
  have ParallaxBackground wrappers, but no images assigned, so they just look
  the same as before (solid dark).
  3. No auto-population — These slots require manual image assignment through
  the admin. They should auto-pull from the gallery/media library instead.

  The fix: Hide empty showcases entirely until images are assigned, and
  auto-populate them from existing imported media so the homepage looks good
  immediately.

  Want me to fix this now — either by hiding empty showcases or by
  auto-populating from your imported projects?

✻ Churned for 41s

❯ no, i added images so you can see. the spacing makes no sense, and there are  
random galleries of images without text attached in a thoughtful way.           

⏺ You're right. I just dropped random image grids between sections without any
  design thinking. A photography company's homepage shouldn't have orphaned
  image blocks floating between copy sections — every visual element needs to
  serve the narrative.

  The better approach: integrate images INTO the existing sections, not between
  them. For example:

  - SpecPlus section already has a split layout with an image slot — that's the
  right pattern
  - BuiltForBuilders should have a powerful full-bleed photo behind the text,
  not a separate image block below it
  - The trio/filmstrip/masonry blocks I added have no context, no heading, no
  connection to the story

  Let me remove the orphaned showcase blocks and instead make the existing
  sections more visually immersive. The parallax backgrounds on the dark
  sections are the right idea — those just need images assigned. The random
  grids between sections should go.

⏺ I see the problems clearly on a 27" screen:

  1. Text is tiny — headings and body text don't scale up for large viewports.
  The max-w-3xl container is too narrow on a wide screen, and the font sizes cap
   at lg: breakpoint (1024px) with no xl: or 2xl: scaling.
  2. Massive empty white space — the copy-only sections (AssetVsContent,
  MarketingEcosystem) have huge dead zones because min-h-[70vh] on a 27" screen
  is enormous with only 3 lines of small text centered in it.
  3. Split sections feel tiny — the SpecPlus/VirtualServices images and text are
   constrained to max-w-6xl which leaves huge margins on wide screens.

  Let me fix the typography scaling and section sizing for large displays.
